topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy paper felt alien under my fingertips. It was a meticulously bound volume, a complete life story, detailing every triumph and stumble of my existence. My breath hitched, a shallow, sharp intake of air that burned my throat. I flipped through the pages, the familiar names and dates blurring into an unfamiliar script. There, in the third chapter, was a memory of a conversation I’d never had, a detail about my childhood that felt surgically inserted. A tremor started in my hands, a frantic need to check the margins, to find the seam where the fabrication began. Every shadow in the room seemed to shift, elongated and watchful. I slammed the book shut, the sound echoing too loudly in the silence. Who had done this? And more terrifyingly, how much did they know?

[story 2]

I kept glancing over my shoulder, expecting a figure to materialize from the periphery of my vision. The biographical sketch lay spread across the antique desk, its carefully crafted narrative mocking me. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the mahogany. I reread a passage describing my greatest achievement—a feat I hadn't even attempted—and a cold, clammy sensation crawled up my spine. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic drumbeat demanding an explanation. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of the room, my footsteps sharp and uneven on the hardwood floor. I kept catching glimpses of reflections in the darkened window, wondering if the shape staring back was the author, or perhaps something else entirely.

[story 3]

The ink seemed to writhe on the page, mocking my attempts to rationalize what I was seeing. I traced a line of text with my finger, the texture of the paper rough beneath my touch. A sudden, intense urge to destroy the object washed over me, a desperate need to erase the evidence. My jaw tightened until it ached, and I swallowed hard, fighting the urge to scream. Every ambient noise—the distant traffic, the hum of the refrigerator—seemed amplified, every sound a potential clue, a whisper from the unseen orchestrator. I backed away slowly, my eyes darting around the apartment as if expecting the words on the page to suddenly spring to life and chase me.
